metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `--ticket` requires a new CLI argument on `_review_code` (add it to Scope if so, and specify how it threads through `shim.review_code` / `CodeReviewRequest` to the `relates_to` emit), or remove it from AC5 and rely solely on the head-commit `rebar-ticket:` trailer for the relates_to link.
findings.finding: The plan's 'relates_to' link mechanism references 'from --ticket' as a source of the work ticket to link, but the _review_code CLI parser has no --ticket argument and the Scope section does not add one. The only path that currently resolves a ticket from a local review is the head commit's rebar-ticket: trailer (via commit_message already threaded through CodeReviewRequest), so the '--ticket' source is a dangling reference with no implementation path specified.
findings.location: What section — 'relates_to link (best-effort)' paragraph; AC5; Scope — _cli/_llm_commands.py
findings.suggested_fix: Since `target_ticket` is already a `CodeReviewRequest` field and the caller (`_review_code`) can already supply it, either: (a) add `--ticket` to the `_review_code` parser and thread it as `target_ticket` (the relates-to target, distinct from the session artifact id), requiring renaming the existing field or adding a second one; OR (b) simply rely on the commit-trailer path (already wired via `commit_message`) and drop '--ticket' from the AC5 wording, noting that trailerless reviews link via the trailer only.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly declare a `depends-on: revenued-thickset-dassie` dep (or a combined-delivery guard) and add an AC-level sequencing note clarifying that `build_payload` must read `verdict.get('session_id')` before this story's session_id wiring can satisfy AC1; alternatively, extend `build_payload` in this story's scope if revenued-thickset-dassie may not land first.
findings.finding: The plan sets verdict["session_id"] so that build_payload (in code_review/sidecar.py) picks it up via verdict.get("session_id"), but build_payload currently does NOT read session_id — that field is only added to build_payload by the dependency story revenued-thickset-dassie (still open/unimplemented). The plan states 'reusing the payload/reader shape from revenued-thickset-dassie' but never makes this sequencing dependency explicit nor specifies what happens if revenued-thickset-dassie has not landed: session_id would be silently dropped from every sidecar payload, causing AC1's 'top-level session_id' requirement to silently fail.
findings.location: What section: 'Set session_id on the verdict' / AC1 ('carrying … a top-level session_id') / sidecar.py build_payload lines 64–91
findings.suggested_fix: Add `revenued-thickset-dassie` to the formal `deps` list of this ticket (so the tracker blocks delivery order), and add one sentence to AC1: 'Requires revenued-thickset-dassie's build_payload extension to be present; if landed first, the session_id field will be missing from all payloads.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cardinality assertion to the AC4 proving command: after two sequential runs with the same `REBAR_SESSION_ID`, assert that `rebar list-tickets --type code_review | jq '[.[] | select(.title=="code-review: session:<id>")] | length'` equals exactly 1.
findings.finding: AC4 asserts 'two sequential local reviews under ONE session id append to the SAME artifact (not duplicate tickets)' but no proving command includes a cardinality assertion — an implementation that creates a new ticket on each run (duplicating rather than reusing) would still satisfy a test that only checks artifact content, not artifact count.
findings.location: AC4
findings.suggested_fix: In AC4 or AC6, add: 'assert `rebar list-tickets --type code_review | jq -e '[.[] | select(.title=="code-review: session:$REBAR_SESSION_ID")] | length == 1'` returns exit 0 after two sequential invocations.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the What section that `build_payload` reading `verdict.get("session_id")` is a revenued-thickset-dassie deliverable (not current codebase state), and add an explicit pre-condition that dassie must be merged before this story for AC1 to hold end-to-end.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `build_payload` 'reads `verdict.get("session_id")`' as a present-tense codebase fact, but `sidecar.build_payload` (lines 64–91) currently has no `session_id` field. This field is a deliverable of revenued-thickset-dassie (confirmed open). If this story is implemented before dassie, the sidecar payload will silently drop `session_id` even after this story correctly sets it on the verdict dict.
findings.location: What section — 'The local path sets verdict["session_id"] = session_key so the payload (revenued-thickset-dassie's build_payload, which reads verdict.get("session_id")) carries it top-level'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the What clause to: 'The local path sets `verdict["session_id"] = session_key`; after revenued-thickset-dassie merges, `build_payload` will read this field and carry it top-level. **Pre-condition: this story requires revenued-thickset-dassie to be merged first.**'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add `--ticket` flag wiring to `_review_code` in scope (with the corresponding shim/dispatch threading), or add commit_message extraction from git HEAD in `_review_code` to enable the `rebar-ticket:` trailer path, and list the chosen mechanism explicitly in the Scope section.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the `relates_to` link is resolved from '`--ticket` or the head commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer', but `_review_code` in `_llm_commands.py` (lines 129–184) has no `--ticket` CLI argument and passes no `commit_message` to `llm.review_code`. Neither mechanism for resolving the work ticket exists in the current `_review_code` code path. Adding `--ticket` and/or commit_message resolution is unscoped (not in the Scope file list).
findings.location: What section — 'relates_to link (best-effort). Link the artifact relates_to the resolved work ticket (from --ticket or the head commit's rebar-ticket: trailer)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise AC1 to scope only what this story delivers: 'Local `rebar review-code` emits a `code_review` artifact keyed `session:<id>` carrying a top-level `session_id` (and, after revenued-thickset-dassie merges, the reviewed-file `deps` map).'
findings.finding: AC1 asserts this story's artifact carries 'the reviewed-file deps map' but `deps` is entirely a revenued-thickset-dassie deliverable (adds deps field to build_payload and the deps collector to gate_dispatch). This story's scope contains no deps computation or payload addition. AC1 conflates the two stories' deliverables.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 — 'Local rebar review-code emits a code_review artifact keyed session:<id> carrying the reviewed-file deps map and a top-level session_id'
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC1 into two clauses: (a) this story: session-keyed artifact with session_id; (b) after dassie: deps map carried through build_payload.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `--ticket` to the `_review_code` argparse block in the Scope section if the plan intends CLI ticket-override, or remove the `--ticket` branch and rely solely on the `rebar-ticket:` trailer from the commit message.
findings.finding: The plan references 'the resolved work ticket (from `--ticket` or the head commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer)' for the `relates_to` link, but `_review_code` in `_llm_commands.py` has no `--ticket` argument. Adding `--ticket` is a CLI signature change not listed in the Scope section.
findings.location: What > Wire the local gate entry > `relates_to` link (best-effort)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly mark AC1 (and any test that asserts `session_id` in the payload) as BLOCKED on revenued-thickset-dassie landing first, and add a note in the plan that the `build_payload` extension from that story is a hard prerequisite — not merely a soft dependency — for the session_id field to appear in emitted artifacts.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that build_payload 'reads verdict.get("session_id")' and that the emitted artifact will carry a 'top-level session_id' (AC1), treating this as already available. Inspecting the current sidecar.py build_payload (lines 64–91) confirms session_id is absent from the payload. The field is added by the dependency story revenued-thickset-dassie, which is still open (status: open). The plan names this only as 'Depends on the payload shape + reader (revenued-thickset-dassie)' without stating that AC1 is BLOCKED until that story lands.
findings.location: What section — 'Set session_id on the verdict' bullet; AC1; Scope — 'Depends on the payload shape + reader (revenued-thickset-dassie)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC6) that specifies a concrete end-to-end proving command exercising the actual `rebar review-code` CLI against a real diff with `REBAR_SESSION_ID` set, and asserts — via `rebar list-tickets` or equivalent structured output — that exactly one `code_review` artifact with title `code-review: session:<id>` exists and carries a top-level `session_id` field.
findings.finding: The plan cuts over the local `rebar review-code` path to emit live artifacts (new production behavior), but no acceptance criterion specifies an end-to-end CLI-level proving command that exercises this path as it will run in production. AC6 ('Tests: a local review with a set REBAR_SESSION_ID emits/reuses one session-keyed artifact across two runs') implies tests but does not require or describe any invocation of the actual `rebar review-code` CLI against a real diff — an agent could satisfy all ACs with mocks/stubs that bypass the new emission path entirely.
findings.location: AC6 / Tests bullet; Scope section (gate_dispatch.py local-emit helper)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6: 'End-to-end smoke: run `REBAR_SESSION_ID=test-sess rebar review-code --ticket <T> <diff>` twice; assert `rebar list-tickets --type code_review | jq -e '[.[] | select(.title=="code-review: session:test-sess")] | length == 1'` and `jq -e '.session_id == "test-sess"' <artifact-sidecar>` — both must pass without error.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test scenarios to AC6 (or the test spec) covering: (a) happy path — a review with `--ticket` resolves and sets the `relates_to` link on the artifact; (b) failure/absent path — a trailerless/ticketless review persists successfully with no link and no exception propagated.
findings.finding: AC6's test plan is entirely silent on the `relates_to`-link behavior introduced in AC5: neither the happy path (link is set when a ticket is available) nor the failure/absent-ticket path (trailerless review still persists, link failure is swallowed) has a corresponding test scenario. This is a new user-facing behavior with zero test coverage in the plan.
findings.location: AC6 (test plan) vs. AC5 (relates_to behavior)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC6 with: 'When `--ticket` is supplied or a `rebar-ticket:` trailer is present, the emitted artifact carries a `relates_to` link to that ticket. When no ticket is resolvable, the artifact is still created without a link and no exception escapes the local review path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the local-emit helper replaces or supplements the existing `_run_code_review_gate` `target_ticket`-gated `sidecar.emit` call, and add an explicit mutual-exclusion rule (e.g., the helper emits post-verdict and the existing gated path is skipped when `session_id` is the emit source, or the helper is placed AFTER `produce_code_review_verdict` returns rather than inside `_run_code_review_gate`).
findings.finding: The new local-emit helper (create-or-reuse + sidecar.emit) is placed inside gate_dispatch.py, but _run_code_review_gate already contains a conditional sidecar.emit call at line 592–595 that fires whenever request.target_ticket is set. The plan threads a resolved artifact_id as target_ticket into CodeReviewRequest — which means BOTH the new local-emit path AND the existing target_ticket-gated emit would fire for a local review, doubling the sidecar event on the artifact. The plan never specifies a suppression protocol or a mutual-exclusion rule between the two emit paths.
findings.location: What section: 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist' / gate_dispatch.py lines 592–595 (_run_code_review_gate existing sidecar.emit)
findings.suggested_fix: Place the local-emit helper in `_review_code` (the CLI entry point) AFTER `shim.review_code` returns the verdict — this keeps it fully out of `_run_code_review_gate` and avoids any interaction with the existing target_ticket-gated emit inside that function.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the MCP `review_code` path (`src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py`) as either in-scope (add session-id resolution + forwarding there too) or explicitly out-of-scope with a rationale.
findings.finding: The MCP `review_code` tool in `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py` calls `rebar.llm.review_code(base, head, reviewers, ref, source)` and is an unenumerated caller of `shim.review_code`. The plan adds `session_id` and `target_ticket` params to the shim but never addresses whether the MCP path should also resolve a session id and emit a local artifact, or is intentionally excluded.
findings.location: Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the 'What' section whether the local-emit helper should **call** `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` or **replicate** its logic in `gate_dispatch.py` — if replication, say so explicitly (e.g., 'duplicate the create-or-reuse logic from voter.py into a new `_local_emit_code_review` helper in gate_dispatch.py').
findings.finding: The 'What' section instructs the implementer to mirror `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` (voter.py ~199-220) 'EXACTLY' when building the local-emit helper in `gate_dispatch.py`, while the 'Scope' section declares the voter path OUT OF SCOPE. This creates cross-section ambiguity: should the implementer (a) call the existing voter function directly (coupling gate_dispatch to voter) or (b) duplicate the logic in gate_dispatch.py? The plan intends (b), but does not say so explicitly, and an implementer who reads 'mirror EXACTLY' as 'call the same function' would introduce an unintended coupling.
findings.location: What section, 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist' bullet ('mirroring voter.emit_code_review_artifact... EXACTLY') vs. Scope section ('OUT of scope: the Gerrit voter path')
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'mirroring voter.emit_code_review_artifact (voter.py ~199–220) EXACTLY' to 'replicating the create-or-reuse logic from voter.emit_code_review_artifact into a new standalone helper in gate_dispatch.py (do NOT call voter.py directly)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or expand AC6) to explicitly verify that `_review_code` in `_llm_commands.py` calls `resolve_session_id()` and uses the result as the session key — e.g., 'when `REBAR_SESSION_ID` is set, the artifact title contains that value; when no session env var is set, the title contains a uuid'.
findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly lists `_llm_commands.py` as in-scope for 'resolve key, thread it down', but no AC directly verifies that `_review_code` in `_llm_commands.py` calls `resolve_session_id()` or threads the key into the call chain. AC1 provides implicit end-to-end coverage, but an implementer writing a targeted unit test for `_llm_commands.py` has no AC to anchor against, leaving the key-resolution step untestable in isolation.
findings.location: Scope section (`_llm_commands.py` bullet) vs. AC6 (tests section)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the scan-then-create TOCTOU with an atomic upsert — either via a unique-title constraint enforced server-side, an advisory lock around the scan+create, or a retry-on-duplicate strategy — so concurrent same-session invocations cannot create duplicate artifacts (AC4).
findings.finding: The create-or-reuse idempotency (scan `list_tickets` for title match, then `create_ticket` if not found) is a check-then-act TOCTOU race: two concurrent local-review invocations sharing the same `REBAR_SESSION_ID` (e.g., parallel sub-agents) can both scan, both find nothing, and both create, producing duplicate `code_review` artifacts — directly contradicting AC4.
findings.location: What section — 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' bullet; AC4
findings.suggested_fix: After the `create_ticket` call, catch a duplicate-title conflict error (if the backend exposes one) and re-resolve to the existing artifact. Alternatively, wrap the scan+create in a file-backed advisory lock scoped to `session:<key>` so only one invocation creates while others wait and then reuse.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup path for session-keyed artifacts (e.g., TTL, a periodic pruning step, or a maximum-age policy) and explicitly document that uuid4-keyed artifacts from bare invocations are ephemeral/purgeable.
findings.finding: There is no RETIRE or cleanup path for session-keyed `code_review` artifacts, and the uuid4 fallback (one unique key per bare invocation) creates a new artifact on every bare run with no retirement mechanism — leading to unbounded artifact accumulation.
findings.location: What section — 'Fallback (approach A)' bullet; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extract the shared create-or-reuse idempotency logic from `voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact` into a shared helper (e.g. `sidecar._ensure_session_artifact(session_key, repo_root)` or a new `code_review/artifact.py` utility) so both the local-emit path and the Gerrit voter path call one implementation instead of duplicating ~50 lines.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a new 'local-emit helper' in `gate_dispatch.py` that re-implements the create-or-reuse idempotency logic already present verbatim in `voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact` (~50 lines: scan `list_tickets(ticket_type='code_review')` for a title match, reuse or create via `rebar.create_ticket`, call `sidecar.emit()`, and add `relates_to` links, all wrapped in best-effort). The plan itself acknowledges this by saying 'mirroring voter.emit_code_review_artifact (voter.py ~199–220) EXACTLY', yet proposes duplication rather than extraction.
findings.location: What section — 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist' / 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' paragraph; voter.py lines 169–270
findings.suggested_fix: Move the create-or-reuse + sidecar.emit + relates_to block from `voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact` into a helper such as `rebar.llm.code_review.sidecar.ensure_artifact(title, description, verdict, *, repo_root, relates_to_refs=())` that both voter.py and the new local-emit path call. The Gerrit voter and the local path differ only in their title scheme and the source of `relates_to` refs — both can be threaded as arguments.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing to duplication, evaluate extracting the create-or-reuse + relates_to logic from `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` into a shared helper (e.g. `code_review/artifact.py::ensure_code_review_artifact(title, verdict, *, repo_root, commit_message='')`) parametrised on the title string, and have both voter and the new local path call it — or explicitly justify in the plan why duplication is preferable (e.g. module-coupling constraints).
findings.finding: The plan proposes duplicating the create-or-reuse idempotency logic from voter.emit_code_review_artifact (voter.py ~199–270: scan list_tickets by title, create-or-reuse via rebar.create_ticket, sidecar.emit, relates_to links — all best-effort) into a new 'local-emit helper' in gate_dispatch.py, explicitly saying it mirrors that function 'EXACTLY'. This ~50-line pattern already exists in voter.py and is well-tested. The plan gives no reason why the existing function cannot be refactored into a shared helper (e.g. in sidecar.py or a new code_review/artifact.py) parametrised on the title-keying strategy, instead of duplicating it.
findings.location: What section — 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist' / 'Create-or-reuse idempotency (session key = stable convergence point)' paragraph; voter.py lines 169–270
findings.suggested_fix: Extract the idempotency body of voter.emit_code_review_artifact into a new internal helper `_ensure_artifact(title, verdict, *, repo_root, commit_message='', change_id='', revision='', change_fp='') -> str | None` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` (already imported by voter) and have voter call it. The local path then calls the same helper with `title = f'code-review: session:{session_key}'`. This closes the duplication without introducing any new module.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the tie-breaking rule for multiple title matches in the create-or-reuse bullet (e.g., 'select the artifact with the lowest/first id, or the most-recently-modified one') so the executor does not have to decide.
findings.finding: The create-or-reuse idempotency spec ('scan rebar.list_tickets for a title match') does not specify which artifact to select when multiple tickets with the same title exist (e.g., if a prior run created a duplicate due to a race or manual creation). An agent must choose — first match, newest, oldest — with no guidance, making the behavior subtly non-deterministic across implementors.
findings.location: What section — 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the idempotency bullet: 'If multiple artifacts share the title, select the one with the earliest creation timestamp (or lowest id) to be deterministic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CLAUDE.md / ADR entry documenting the new dual local+Gerrit session-keyed emission model and the canonical artifact title format (`code-review: session:{id}`) as the shared create/reuse contract key.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — dual local+Gerrit artifact emission keyed by session ID — but calls for no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry to document this new pipeline stage and its idempotency contract (title format `code-review: session:{id}` as shared create/lookup key).
findings.location: ## What (Wire the local gate entry) + ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Update CLAUDE.md (or create an ADR) documenting the session-keyed dual-path emission model: the canonical artifact title format `code-review: session:{session_key}`, the create-or-reuse idempotency invariant, and the distinction between local (session-keyed) and Gerrit (change-keyed) emission paths.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that artifact-emission failures are logged at WARNING level with a structured tag (e.g., `event=code_review_artifact_failure`) so that silent persistence failures are detectable in log aggregation or a counter metric.
findings.finding: Observability of silent persistence failures is underspecified: the plan says failures are 'logged and swallowed' but names no log level, structured field, metric counter, or alert, so operators cannot distinguish a silent artifact-emission failure from a successful one without reading raw logs.
findings.location: What section — 'Best-effort throughout' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Emit `logger.warning('code_review artifact emit failed', extra={'event': 'code_review_artifact_failure', 'session_key': session_key, 'error': str(exc)})` in the swallow-exception block.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `target_ticket` edit: it already exists on `CodeReviewRequest`; only `session_id: str | None = None` needs to be added to the dataclass — remove `target_ticket` from the list of dataclass additions.
findings.finding: `CodeReviewRequest` in `gate_dispatch.py` already has a `target_ticket: str | None = None` field (line 467). The plan's 'Signature changes (in scope)' section says to add `target_ticket` to `CodeReviewRequest` — this is a hallucinated edit that will confuse the implementer.
findings.location: What > Wire the local gate entry > Signature changes (in scope)
findings.suggested_fix: In 'Signature changes (in scope)', change 'add a `session_id: str | None = None` field to the `CodeReviewRequest` dataclass (alongside the existing `target_ticket`)' to 'add only `session_id: str | None = None` to `CodeReviewRequest` — `target_ticket` already exists'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
